title: Cybianthus lineatus
taxonomy: Cybianthus lineatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This family is commonly known as the primrose family. The genus Cybianthus is a group of plants found in Central and South America, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Cybianthus lineatus is further grouped into the order Ericales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Cybianthus lineatus, also known as the 'Tres Marías', is a plant species that has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial for wound healing and reducing inflammation.
